occurrence_sentence: 5.2 Establish a quantum computing council for Saudi Arabia

interaction_phrase: commissioned by
occurrence_sentence: This report was commissioned by the Ministry of Communications and Information Technology (MCIT), Kingdom of Saudi Arabia (KSA) on terms specifically limiting the liability of Arthur D. Little.

interaction_phrase: provided to us by
occurrence_sentence: Our conclusions are the results of the exercise of our best professional judgement, based in part upon materials and information provided to us by MCIT, secondary research, and expert interviews conducted by Arthur D. Little.

interaction_phrase: priority for prominent research universities
occurrence_sentence: quantum computing (QC) is moving quickly from an interesting, emerging technology to a priority for prominent research universities, pioneering companies across various industries as well as forward-looking governments.

interaction_phrase: leading QC research
occurrence_sentence: Significant technological breakthroughs are frequently being announced by universities and companies leading QC research, and an increasing number of patents are being filed every year across the world, demonstrating an exponential progress towards QC maturity.

interaction_phrase: First included in Gartner's Emerging Technologies Hype Cycle
occurrence_sentence: First included in Gartner's Emerging Technologies Hype Cycle in 2005, the quantum promise has been growing steadily.

interaction_phrase: Between 2.5 days (according to IBM) and 10,000 years (according to Google)
occurrence_sentence: Between 2.5 days (according to IBM) and 10,000 years (according to Google), based on article "On Quantum Supremacy", IBM, October 2019

interaction_phrase: Hybridan LLP, February 2019 Arthur D, Little estimates
occurrence_sentence: While the current technology and fabrication process can be scaled up to ~10,000 qubits7, going beyond that threshold into hundreds of thousands (or even millions of qubits) will require different technology and processes redesign. Despite this, QC hardware manufacturers claim there are ways in which this can be achieved, and do not see it as a fundamental obstacle. R&D will be key to continue progressing towards "quantum supremacy or advantage", i.e. reaching a level at which a quantum computer is superior to a traditional one in terms of time, cost and quality for specific use cases.

interaction_phrase: spending USD 11.4 billion of public investment on the National Laboratory for Quantum Information Services
occurrence_sentence: China's 2017 commitment to spending USD 11.4 billion of public investment on the National Laboratory for Quantum Information Services, which is currently under construction in the Anhui province9, dwarfs the spend of other countries.
target_actor: National Laboratory for Quantum Information Services

interaction_phrase: Leading tech companies such as Google
occurrence_sentence: Leading tech companies such as Google, IBM, Intel and Microsoft are betting significantly on the development of QC, with undisclosed cumulative investments reaching billions of dollars.

interaction_phrase: Leading tech companies such as IBM
occurrence_sentence: Leading tech companies such as Google, IBM, Intel and Microsoft are betting significantly on the development of QC, with undisclosed cumulative investments reaching billions of dollars.

interaction_phrase: Leading private sector players such as Saudi Aramco
occurrence_sentence: Leading private sector players such as Saudi Aramco, which already uses supercomputer systems, could be potential QC investors.

interaction_phrase: have adopted different approaches to QC science development
occurrence_sentence: Universities and private companies across the globe have adopted different approaches to QC science development, ranging from a "full stack" approach to specializing in specific areas.

interaction_phrase: beta QCaaS cloud platforms
occurrence_sentence: Quantum computing-as-a-service (QCaaS) beta platforms are becoming an ideal alternative for businesses and universities, allowing them to leverage QC infrastructure capabilities without having to make significant investments to create, maintain or operate quantum computers.

interaction_phrase: partnering
occurrence_sentence: Private companies and academia are partnering to track and estimate the current and future workforce needs of the quantum industry, and ensure these needs are included in academic curriculums.

interaction_phrase: attract of foreign talent
occurrence_sentence: Countries such as Singapore are looking to attract of foreign talent for QC technological roles by reducing red tape.

interaction_phrase: supported in their efforts by national technology champions such as Alibaba, Microsoft and IBM
occurrence_sentence: Both countries have the financial muscle to back QC R&D with billions of dollars and are also supported in their efforts by national technology champions such as Alibaba, Microsoft and IBM.

interaction_phrase: include academic institutions such as
occurrence_sentence: As well as public entities such as MCIT, this should include academic institutions such as KAUST and industry leaders.

interaction_phrase: the country should define a formal QC coordination body
occurrence_sentence: In order to ensure that KSA takes full advantage of the QC wave, the country should define a formal QC coordination body.

interaction_phrase: Encourage the creation of specific QC disciplines in universities
occurrence_sentence: Encourage the creation of specific QC disciplines in universities, with their own faculty and programs.

interaction_phrase: Leading universities such as KAUST
occurrence_sentence: Leading universities such as KAUST, which already possesses centers including the Shaheen Supercomputing Laboratory, could create a specific QC institute.

interaction_phrase: attract professionals in QC and related fields for KSA's leading universities
occurrence_sentence: Take advantage of visa restrictions in other markets and reduce red tape to attract professionals in QC and related fields for KSA's leading universities and companies.

occurrence_sentence: For example, Japanese telco NTT data has recently established a QC research center in Silicon Valley, and Harbour. Space University in Barcelona, which offers courses of QC cryptography, is sponsored by the Moscow Institute of Technology.
